name: Jimmy Heath
description: American jazz saxophonist, composer, arranger and big band leader (1926–2020)
abstract: James Edward Heath (October 25, 1926 – January 19, 2020), nicknamed Little Bird, was an American jazz saxophonist, composer, arranger, and big band leader. He was the brother of bassist Percy Heath and drummer Albert Heath.

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: I started working with him... So I started working with Jimmy. He had had – his first record... It was a small big band. So I started working with him, playing those arrangements.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Christian McBride
about_quotes.evidence: So I had a chance to meet and play with like Red Rodney and Kenny Barron, Curtis Fuller, Jimmy Heath, Walter Davis Jr.
about_quotes.doc_title: Christian McBride interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Orrin Keepnews
about_quotes.evidence: the second record I did with Jimmy Heath... we did like a ten-piece date... Jimmy Heath album from about 1961
about_quotes.doc_title: Orrin Keepnews interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: Jimmy Heath put a big band together... I was playing fourth tenor... John and I were in the band
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Buster Williams
about_quotes.evidence: one of my first gigs was with Jimmy Heath at one of these cabarets. Jimmy Heath was one band
about_quotes.doc_title: Buster Williams interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Orrin Keepnews
about_quotes.evidence: But people like Jimmy, like Griffin, like Blue Mitchell, these were my friends.
about_quotes.doc_title: Orrin Keepnews — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
